title: Somatosensory evoked potentials in the human spinal cord to mixed nerve stimulation
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous multimodal neurophysiological recordings from 40 participants, including electroencephalography (EEG), electrospinography (ESG), electroneurography (ENG), and electromyography (EMG), along with electrocardiographic and respiratory signals. Data were acquired during four conditions: resting state with eyes open, mixed median nerve stimulation, mixed tibial nerve stimulation, and alternating mixed nerve stimulation. The dataset enables investigation of somatosensory evoked potentials in the human spinal cord and their cortical correlates. This study was pre-registered on OSF (https://osf.io/sgptzt).
methods_description: Simultaneous EEG-ESG-ENG-EMG recordings were acquired from 40 participants under four conditions: resting state (eyes open), mixed median nerve stimulation (arm), mixed tibial nerve stimulation (leg), and alternating mixed nerve stimulation. Recordings included electrocardiographic and respiratory signals. ESG electrode positions are provided for each participant. The EEG channel Fz was referenced to both the EEG reference (right mastoid, RM) and the ESG reference (6th thoracic vertebra, TH6). This study was pre-registered on OSF: https://osf.io/sgptzt.
